mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict positive 60-day returns (win rate < 50 percent) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term equity price appreciation over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 5-day forward returns during late-cycle phases, with a Rank IC below -0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained federal funds rates above 4.5 percent signal restrictive monetary policy that dampens equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in over 55 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative 60-day return win rate below 45 percent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signal a sharp contraction in residential investment and construction activity, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during housing downturns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of instances where HOUST drops below 1.2 million units.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 20-day returns, with a cumulative Rank IC below 0.01 across all rolling windows.
